page: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on an arm64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz with 1.0 thread per core, this instance features a massive 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory, translating to 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight exceptional multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with Clang. Its memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, reaching 860.6 GB/sec for large-block reads and 409.74 GB/sec for large-block writes. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, executing 355.04 million operations per second.

In contrast, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average tier. LLM inference tasks show mixed results; while prompt processing for medium and large models is strong, text generation speeds are average to poor, with the small 135M model generating only 16.66 tokens per second. This profile indicates high resource density and cost efficiency for highly parallelized, memory-intensive workloads, though it presents a tradeoff for single-threaded or GPU-dependent tasks.
The x8g.metal-48xl is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, massive parallel software compilation, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on an arm64 architecture. Operating at 2.7 GHz, it is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory read/write bandwidth, and superior database and Redis operation speeds. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to poor. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized, memory-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and intensive data processing pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized arm64 server featuring 192 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 3 TB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal arm64 server with 192 dedicated Graviton cores, 3072 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized arm64 platform delivering 192 dedicated cores and three terabytes of DDR5 RAM for massive parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on arm64 architecture
3072.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory read/write bandwidth
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPUs
Best-fit for in-memory databases, Redis, and parallel data processing
